failedAssertions.reason: Representative runtime deny/allow behavior is visible, but `AuthTenantAuthorityIT` still fails because denied privileged/reset actions do not emit the expected `ACCESS_DENIED` audit metadata and the support-reset denial regression remains red.
The dedicated privileged-action matrix is not validation-clean: `AuthTenantAuthorityIT` still reports missing denial audit metadata and one remaining support-reset denial regression.

appliedUpdates.description: Recorded the newer delegated-helper invalid-model failure (`custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high`) and the compose restart overrides required to run the current code on the local auth runtime (`prod,flyway-v2` plus explicit CORS overrides).

environmentConstraints: The delegated `user-testing-flow-validator` helper could not launch because the local Task runner still references `custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high`; validation was completed directly in-session.

A plain `docker compose up -d --build app` is not sufficient for current-code runtime validation on this branch because `.env` forces `SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=dev`, the compose database only has `flyway_schema_history_v2`, and production CORS validation rejects the persisted HTTP origins. The runtime was rebuilt successfully only with explicit profile/CORS overrides.
The rebuilt runtime returned `503 SYS_004 Failed to dispatch email via SMTP` for the super-admin force-reset probe, so live reset-allow-path evidence relied partly on targeted integration tests.
